explanation: The code reads a graph's vertices and edges from a file and constructs an adjacency matrix to represent the graph. It uses depth-first search (DFS) to explore the graph, marking the nodes as visited and detecting cycles. When a cycle is found, it traces back through the graph to identify the nodes involved and outputs the cycle structure. This can be useful in fields like network analysis and algorithm development.
name: 2009-06-21_unnamed
subject: test
summary: This project enumerates all elementary cycles in a graph based on the input provided via a text file.
